raw_consumer_statement:
I am disputing a {$4800.00} credit card charge on my Citi Preferred account. I timely disputed the charge on XX/XX/year>, within the 60-day window under the Fair Credit Billing Act.

The merchant has confirmed in writing that the transaction never settled and no funds were received. Because the charge never settled, no refund or credit slip exists. I paid the merchant through an alternate payment method on XX/XX/year>. 
Citi closed the dispute due to a missed documentation email and is now refusing to correct the billing error, stating that more than 60 days have passed and that California law requires a credit slip. This is incorrect, as a credit slip can not exist for a transaction that never settled. 
Citi is attempting to hold me responsible for a charge that the merchant confirms was never paid.
